name: Datomic Immutable Database API
what_it_does: Immutable functional database with Datalog query API — enabling Clojure and JVM developers to build applications on an immutable, accumulate-only database with built-in time travel queries (as-of, since, history), ACID transactions, and a Datalog query language that treats the entire database as a value, with Datomic Pro (commercial) and Datomic Cloud (AWS).
use_cases: Building immutable data systems via Datomic API for audit-complete application state management
Querying historical data via Datomic as-of API for point-in-time data reconstruction
Implementing event sourcing patterns via Datomic transactions for domain event logging
Building Clojure applications via Datomic for functional database operations
Managing schema evolution via Datomic for additive schema changes with full history
not_for: Non-JVM/non-Clojure stacks (Datomic is primarily JVM/Clojure-centric with limited other clients)
High-throughput OLTP with millions of writes/second (Datomic is not an OLTP database)
Teams unfamiliar with Datalog or functional programming concepts
best_when: A Clojure or JVM team wants an immutable, functional database with time-travel queries and a Datalog query model — especially for applications where complete audit history and replayability are first-class requirements.
avoid_when: You're not using Clojure/JVM, need high write throughput, or prefer SQL-based temporal databases.

auth.notes: No REST API — Datomic uses peer library (JVM in-process) or client library (Datomic Cloud). Peer model: Datomic Pro — Java/Clojure peer library connects directly to transactor. Client model: Datomic Cloud — client library connects to Datomic ions (AWS Lambda functions). Datomic Cloud: AWS IAM credentials for authentication. Datomic Pro: transactor connection string (datomic:dev://, datomic:sql://, etc.). No HTTP API by default — pure library-based access. Ion functions: Datomic Cloud serverless functions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Datomic Free (legacy): limited, deprecated; Datomic Pro: 30-day trial
pricing.paid_tiers: Datomic Pro: $4,995/year (small); Enterprise: custom; Datomic Cloud: AWS instance costs
pricing.notes: US company. Cognitect (acquired by Nubank 2020). Created by Rich Hickey (Clojure creator). Nubank (Brazilian fintech): owns Cognitect and Datomic. Free in 2023: Cognitect/Nubank made Datomic Pro free (October 2023). Datomic Cloud: runs on AWS, requires AWS account. Architecture: Transactor (writes) + Storage (DynamoDB/SQL) + Peers (reads). Immutability: facts (datoms) never retracted — only added (excision for special cases). As-of: query database as it was at any past time. History: query all values ever held by an attribute. Ions: deploy Datomic applications as serverless functions. Datalog: Clojure-style logic queries over entities. Rich Hickey's design: 'value-based' database — database as immutable value.

performance.rate_limits: Single transactor: ~5K transactions/second; reads scale horizontally via peers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: NO REST API — JVM peer library or Cloud client required. Datalog query language. Clojure/JVM only (practically). Made free 2023 by Nubank. AWS required for Datomic Cloud. Transactor single-writer architecture. As-of/history/since temporal APIs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Immutable design: all reads are idempotent (database as value). Transactions: idempotent via transaction IDs. datoms (facts) accumulate — same fact transacted twice is idempotent (deduplication by datom uniqueness).
known_agent_gotchas: NO REST API: Datomic uses peer library (JVM) or Cloud client — not HTTP-based integration
Clojure/JVM only (practically): Java SDK exists but Clojure is primary and best-supported
Datomic Cloud requires AWS: runs on your AWS account (EC2 instances, DynamoDB) — significant setup
Single transactor: writes are single-threaded through one transactor — plan for write throughput limits
Made free (Oct 2023): Datomic Pro is now free — update pricing assumptions accordingly
Nubank acquisition: Brazilian fintech owns Cognitect — verify long-term product commitment

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (JVM SSL/TLS). AWS IAM for Datomic Cloud (strong cloud security). Immutable design: past data cannot be modified — strong audit trail. Excision (delete) requires special permission. GDPR: excision exists but is complex — plan GDPR compliance before adoption. Nubank (Brazilian fintech): strict regulatory environment drives security standards. Peer model: peers have read access to all data — carefully manage peer permissions. Single transactor: write access control via transactor credentials.
